chapter_title.en: Chapter on Judgment Regarding the Enclosure Between Two Houses

text: 3412 - سَأَلَ مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ حَظِيرَةٍ بَيْنَ دَارَيْنِ فَذَكَرَ "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَضَى بِهَا لِصَاحِبِ اَلدَّارِ اَلَّذِي مِنْ قِبَلِهِ اَلْقِمَاطُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3412 - Mansur ibn Hazim asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a courtyard situated between two houses.
Imam (as) mentioned that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that it belongs to the owner of the house from whose side the boundary rope (al-qimat) extends.
